name: Embalse de José Torán y Mirador Las Palomas — circular desde La Puebla de los Infantes
region_intro: Jogging around La Puebla De Los Infantes offers diverse landscapes at the foot of the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The region features varied topography, transitioning from mountains to the fertile plains of the Guadalquivir Valley. Key natural features include the José Torán Reservoir, the Toril Stream, and the Santa Lucía Valley with its orange groves. This mid-mountain terrain is characterized by olive groves and pine forests, providing a range of running environments. Best jogging routes around La Puebla De Los Infantes The most popular jogging route is Embalse de José Torán y Mirador Las Palomas — circular desde La Puebla de los Infantes, a 12.5 miles (20.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 24 minutes to complete. This difficult route circles the José Torán Reservoir and leads to the Mirador Las Palomas viewpoint. Another top favourite among local runners is Race along the Guadalquivir on the old path between Cordoba and Seville, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This route follows the Guadalquivir river, offering views of the plains. Local runners also love the Las Navas de la Concepción loop from Las Navas de la Concepción, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through natural surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 17 minutes. Jogging around La Puebla De Los Infantes is defined by the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, the José Torán Reservoir, and the Guadalquivir Valley. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 80 runners have used komoot to explore La Puebla De Los Infantes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: There are over 9 diverse running routes around La Puebla De Los Infantes, catering to various fitness levels. These trails explore the varied landscapes from the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park to the Guadalquivir Valley.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced joggers?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Hornachuelos&lt;/a&gt; offer a demanding experience. This difficult route spans approximately 20.5 km with significant elevation changes, providing a rigorous workout amidst scenic surroundings.

Q: Can I find any easy or beginner-friendly jogging paths in the area?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for an easier pace, the region offers gentle paths. While specific beginner routes are not detailed, the area's diverse terrain includes sections suitable for a relaxed run, particularly along flatter sections of the Guadalquivir plains.

Q: What kind of natural attractions or landmarks can I see while jogging?
A: Jogging routes in La Puebla De Los Infantes often feature stunning natural beauty. You can encounter the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564942" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;José Torán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4352235" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Lucía Valley&lt;/a&gt; with its orange groves, and various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/564940" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de Las Palomas&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the surrounding natural park and the Guadalquivir plains.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Cortijo del Pingarillo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging circular route covering about 22.6 km, perfect for a long-distance run.

Q: Is the area suitable for family jogging or running with children?
A: The diverse landscape of La Puebla De Los Infantes includes sections that are more accessible and less strenuous, making them suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not highlighted, easier paths along the Guadalquivir river or around the reservoir's flatter sections can be enjoyed by all ages.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: The mid-mountain terrain and varied vegetation make spring and autumn particularly pleasant for jogging, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be enjoyable, especially on shaded routes through pine forests or near the reservoir.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park?
A: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's important to keep them on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always check local regulations for specific routes within the park.

Q: Where can I park when accessing the jogging routes?
A: Parking is typically available near the starting points of many routes, especially in and around La Puebla De Los Infantes and near popular access points to the José Torán Reservoir. Specific parking details may vary by route, so it's advisable to check the route description before your visit.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the region?
A: For those looking for longer runs, the area provides several options. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20122708" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hornachuelos Old Town loop from Cortijo del Pingarillo&lt;/a&gt;, at approximately 22.6 km, offer a substantial distance for endurance training or a lengthy scenic run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in La Puebla De Los Infantes?
A: Over 80 runners have explored the routes around La Puebla De Los Infantes using komoot. They often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from the challenging mountain trails of the Sierra Norte to the serene paths around the José Torán Reservoir and the scenic views of the Guadalquivir Valley.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs near the jogging routes for a post-run refreshment?
A: La Puebla De Los Infantes, being a charming mountain town, offers local establishments where you can enjoy refreshments after your run. Many routes start or pass near the town, providing convenient access to cafes and pubs.
tips: The Mirador de Las Palomas is a spectacular place located in La Puebla de los Infantes, in the province of Seville. From the viewpoint you can see the reservoir, which is an ideal place for fishing and bird watching.

The José Torán Reservoir is located in the bed of the Arroyo Guadalbarcar/Víbora river, forming part of the municipalities of Constantina, Lora del Río and Puebla de los Infantes, in the province of Seville in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park.
The dam is easily accessible from Lora del Río, shortly after passing the Castle of Setefilla, the reservoir is located about 12 km from this town.
Beautiful Sevillian town that is close to the Sierra León and with numerous churches in its center. The cobbled streets in the town center give it a special touch.
